invalid_review_attempts_not_counted.reason: Claude CLI process produced a zero-byte output file after an extended wait and was terminated; no result, session id, or structured_output was available.

findings.title: Forbidden claim `increment_retry_attempt` is declared but never validator-checked or test-exercised
findings.description: `increment_retry_attempt` is listed in `CONTROL_PLANE_PERMISSION_AUDIT_BINDING_RETRY_BEHAVIOR_POLICY.forbiddenRetryBehaviorClaims`, but unlike every sibling retry claim it is absent from both the policy validator's expected forbidden-claims loop in validators.js (assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehaviorPolicy) and the fail-closed test loop in service.test.js ('fails closed on unsafe claims and wrong source'). It is still rejected at runtime by the generic forbidden-claim handling, so this is not a safety gap, only an unverified declaration that could be silently dropped in a future edit without any test catching it.
findings.recommendation: Add `increment_retry_attempt` to the validator's expected forbiddenRetryBehaviorClaims assertion loop and to the fail-closed test's claim array (one line each) so the declared forbidden claim is pinned by both the contract validator and the unit tests.

validation_notes: Confirmed metadata-only safety posture: precheckControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehavior performs no I/O/execution and returns a frozen receipt; all *_permitted flags (retry enqueue/execution/schedule write/attempt record/timer/worker, rollback, compensation, operator, customer, incident, observability) are hard-false; no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false.

Confirmed micro-phase RP00.P02.M05 stays open (policy.completesSourceMicroPhase=false, result.source_micro_phase_completed=false) and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S19; status 'production_ready' is the project contract-first convention and the policy explicitly disclaims retry execution, so it is not a false readiness claim.

Confirmed fail-closed behavior: any truthy claim (forbidden or unknown) throws; input gating rejects missing/wrong S17 rollback result, wrong entrypoint, missing rollback receipt, and missing/incorrect carried retry_strategy_ref before building the receipt.

Confirmed carry-forward integrity: assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRetryBehaviorResult re-validates the carried S17 result by overriding working_product_behavior_ref/next_subphase back to S17 values, so carried fields cannot drift while passing S18.

Confirmed RP00 validator advancement: currentContractSubphaseId moved to S18, RP00.P02.M05.S17 added to requiredClosedSubphases, and new contract-definition binding/closeout-requirement checks added for control_plane_permission_audit_binding_retry_behavior.
Treated the expected RP00 validator failure (missing docs/closeout-packs/cp00-014 files) as out of scope per review instructions; not counted as a finding.

Could not inspect the full contract (+661) and fixture (+790) JSON diffs (truncated after 90000 chars); relied on the reported passing project validators (validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, spec:requirements:validate, goal:closeout:validate, closeout-pack:validate CP00-001..013) plus 409 control-plane and 493 total tests for contract/fixture/ledger consistency.

Receipt note (non-finding): retry_behavior_receipt carries an extra diagnostic field decision_reason not enumerated in requiredRetryBehaviorReceiptFields; this is consistent with the prior S17 pattern and is explicitly asserted, so it is correct, not a defect.
raw_review.summary: CP00-014 closes RP00.P02.M05.S18 (Permission And Audit Binding "Retry behavior") as a metadata-only boundary following the established S10–S17 pattern. The new policy, prechecker, precheck function, asserts, getter, tests, fixture mirror, and RP00 validator advancement are internally consistent and safe: the precheck is a pure function returning a frozen synthetic retry-behavior receipt with no I/O, no execution, and all write/execution permission flags hard-false (retry enqueue/execution/schedule/attempt/timer/worker, plus carried rollback/compensation/customer/incident/observability). It is no-real-data, no-product-write, keeps RP00.P02.M05 open (completesSourceMicroPhase=false), fails closed on both forbidden and unknown truthy claims, strictly gates on the S17 rollback source (entrypoint, rollback_behavior_subphase_id, receipt presence, carried retry_strategy_ref), and re-validates carried S17 fields via an override-based re-assert. No P0/P1 blocker would make S18 unsafe or falsely production_ready. One minor P2 hygiene gap: the forbidden claim increment_retry_attempt is declared but neither validator-checked nor test-exercised. The expected RP00 validator failure for missing docs/closeout-packs/cp00-014 files was treated as out of scope per instructions; the full 661-line contract and 790-line fixture diffs were truncated, so contract/fixture/ledger consistency was confirmed via the passing project validators rather than line-by-line.

adjudication_notes: One completed claude-opus-4-8 max read-only/no-tools review is recorded for CP00-014.
Claude returned PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with zero P0 and zero P1 findings.
The single raw P2 finding about increment_retry_attempt claim pinning was fixed by adding explicit validator and test coverage after review, then rerunning targeted validation.
The expected pre-evidence RP00 failure is resolved by creating this CP00-014 evidence and rerunning final validation.
